name: Zendesk API
what_it_does: Zendesk's REST API for managing support tickets, customers, agents, organizations, and knowledge base articles in Zendesk's customer service platform.
use_cases: Creating and updating support tickets from agent workflows
Automatically routing and assigning tickets based on content
Reading ticket data for SLA monitoring and escalation agents
Managing knowledge base articles for AI-powered self-service
Building AI copilots for agent support using ticket context
not_for: Real-time in-app chat-first support (Intercom may be better)
Very small teams where cost is prohibitive
Simple internal IT ticketing (JIRA Service Desk may be better)
best_when: An agent needs to manage customer support tickets in Zendesk as part of an automated support or AIOps workflow.
avoid_when: You're not on Zendesk or need chat-first customer communication.

auth.notes: API tokens with email/token Basic Auth (agent@domain.com/token:API_TOKEN). OAuth2 for multi-account apps. API tokens are simplest for agents — no OAuth flow needed.

pricing.paid_tiers: Suite Team $55/agent/month, Suite Growth $89/agent/month, Suite Professional $115/agent/month
pricing.notes: Relatively expensive per-seat pricing. No permanent free tier. Startup program available. API access included on all plans.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with description and error fields. Field validation errors are specific. Rate limit errors include rate limit headers. One of the better-documented APIs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: External ID field on tickets enables idempotent creation checks. Ticket updates by ID are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Basic Auth format is unusual: agent_email@domain/token:API_TOKEN — easy to misconfigure
Subdomain-based URLs (company.zendesk.com) require knowing the Zendesk subdomain
Bulk ticket operations use side-loading and job status polling — complex for agents
Webhook registration requires SSL endpoint with valid certificate
Custom fields require knowing their IDs which are numeric and account-specific
Rate limits apply per Zendesk account, not per token — shared with other integrations
